activities_projects.title: Breaking Chalk Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand that matter is made of tiny particles
steps: Take a stick of chalk and break it into two pieces
Continue breaking the pieces into smaller parts
Grind the small pieces using mortar and pestle
Observe the powder using a magnifying glass
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that matter can be broken into smaller particles but still remains the same substance
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation and explanation of particulate nature of matter (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sugar Dissolving in Water
activities_projects.objective: Understand that particles of matter occupy spaces between them
steps: Fill a glass tumbler with water
Add two teaspoons of sugar
Taste water from the top layer before stirring
Stir until sugar dissolves completely
Taste the water again
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that sugar particles dissolve and spread throughout water
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of dissolution and particle distribution (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Observing Properties of Solids
activities_projects.objective: Study the properties of solids and strong particle attraction
steps: Collect different solid objects
Observe their shapes and sizes
Try hammering them carefully
Discuss which objects have strongly packed particles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that solids have fixed shape and strong interparticle attraction
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification and explanation of solid properties (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Water in Different Containers
activities_projects.objective: Understand that liquids take the shape of their container
steps: Mark 200 mL level in each container
Fill the first container with water up to the mark
Pour the water into the second container
Then pour into the third container
Observe the shape and level of water
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that liquids have fixed volume but no fixed shape

activities_projects.title: Smoke Spreading in Gas Jars
activities_projects.objective: Understand that gases spread and occupy available space
steps: Light an incense stick to create smoke
Trap smoke in the first gas jar
Cover the jar with a glass plate
Place another jar on top and remove the plate
Observe smoke spreading into the second jar
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that gas particles move freely and fill available space
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation and explanation of gas behavior (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Air Compression Using Syringe
steps: Pull the plunger outward to fill the syringe with air
Close the nozzle with your thumb
Push the plunger inward slowly
Observe the change in volume of air
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that gases have large spaces between particles

activities_projects.title: Sugar Dissolution Level Observation
activities_projects.objective: Show that particles of one substance occupy spaces between particles of another
steps: Fill half a glass vessel with water and mark the level
Add two teaspoons of sugar
Stir with a glass rod until sugar dissolves
Observe the final water level
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about interparticle spaces in liquids

activities_projects.title: Potassium Permanganate Diffusion
activities_projects.objective: Observe movement of particles in liquids
steps: Fill a tumbler with water
Add a few grains of potassium permanganate
Observe the spreading of purple color
Note how it spreads without stirring
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand diffusion and continuous movement of particles

activities_projects.title: Diffusion in Hot and Cold Water
activities_projects.objective: Study the effect of temperature on particle movement
steps: Fill the three tumblers with hot, room temperature, and cold water
Add potassium permanganate crystals to each
Observe the rate of color spreading
Compare the speed in each case
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that higher temperature increases particle movement

activities_projects.title: Incense Stick Fragrance Experiment
steps: Light an incense stick in one corner of the room
Wait for a few minutes
Observe how the fragrance spreads throughout the room
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that gas particles move freely and spread in all directions
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of diffusion of gases (10 marks)
